item.title: Royal Mail boss hauled before Business and Trade Committee
item.description: Our post is broken. And our community is suffering for it. Missing hospital letters. Lost prescriptions. Court documents going astray. I've been hearing it on doorsteps right across the constituency. So, the Commons Business and Trade Committee, which I chair, hauled in the Royal Mail's new owner. He’s on notice. He has committed to publishing an improvement plan. I'll be watching - and reporting every step back to you. Read my column in the latest Solihull Observer:...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: SMALL BUSINESS STRATEGY PUBLISHED
item.description: Small businesses across Hodge Hill and Solihull North, across the country, are facing pandemic-level pressures but without the emergency support. The Business and Trade Committee report published today exposes: £112bn owed in unpaid invoices 38 stores closing on high streets every day Energy costs nearly doubled since 2021 Crime adding 10p to every shop transaction. Local cafés need to sell hundreds more items monthly just to survive. Hair salons face £25,000 extra costs. High streets...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Pavement Parking Update
item.description: Update on Pavement Parking Following up on the pavement parking video I shared earlier this week, I’ve now received a written response from the Transport Minister, Lillian Greenwood MP, which you can read below Why this matters: In Hodge Hill and Solihull North, blocked pavements aren’t a nuisance — they’re a daily barrier for disabled residents, older people, and parents with pushchairs. What the Minister has confirmed: • New powers for councils to tackle pavement obstruction from...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Making the case for Heartlands Hospital Refurbishment
item.description: It was great to meet the Health Minister, Karin Smyth MP, to make the case loud and clear for a new urgent and emergency care department at Heartlands Hospital. Plans are now in development for what would be a transformational investment in local NHS services. It is a huge project, but a vital one. Every day, NHS staff at Heartlands work round the clock, performing absolute miracles under intense pressure. They deserve facilities that match their skill, commitment and dedication - and our...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Another Successful Smith's Wood Residents' Meeting!
item.description: Thank you to everyone who came to our recent Smith’s Wood residents’ meeting. It was a constructive discussion about the priorities we need to fix together in the area. Residents raised concerns about littering, speeding, parking outside schools and safety at bus stops. We also discussed neighbourhood watches, community litter picks and practical steps to improve home and street security, working closely with the police. If you were invited but couldn’t attend, please do return your survey....
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Did Evri bosses mislead the House of Commons?
item.description: Did Evri bosses mislead House of Commons - while couriers were paid below the minimum wage? After the Business and Trade Committee hearings with Evri earlier this year, BBC Panorama investigated further. Their work has raised serious questions about the company’s working practices, including claims that some couriers may have earned well below the legal minimum wage. If Parliament was given one picture while workers lived another, that’s a serious failure of accountability. Working people pay...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Glebe Farm and North Garrets Green Regeneration Win
item.description: Could you help lead this programme? Last year, our community effort delivered something truly significant. We secured a once-in-a-generation win for Glebe Farm and north Garrets Green, with a £20 million investment from the Government’s Pride in Place regeneration programme. This money will help renew the area after years of lack of investment, creating a nicer environment and better prospects for the next generation. With this opportunity comes responsibility, and we’re now looking for...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Dream Chaser Youth Club
item.description: It was an absolute pleasure to visit Dream Chaser last month and to spend time with Birmingham’s very own Pride of Britain Award winner, Asha Rage. The team is doing an outstanding job creating a safe, friendly and genuinely accessible space where young people can get involved in sport, build confidence and feel supported. Places like this offer so much more than physical activity. They give young people a sense of belonging, positive role models and opportunities to thrive. Seeing the impact...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Thank you & Happy Christmas from the Business & Trade Committee
item.description: As we break for recess, I today wrote to all MPs to say a huge thank you for supporting the Business and Trade Committee’s work this year! Thanks to their help and to our extremely hard-working Committee members and Committee team, we’ve been able to deliver thirteen reports and inquiries this year which I thought I’d share in case you’d like some recess reading! We kicked off as promised with our stock-take on the Post Office Horizon Scandal ; published on New Year’s Day this was widely...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: BUDGET WINS FOR HODGE HILL & SOLIHULL NORTH
item.description: Today’s Budget sets out a series of commitments that will make a real difference for families across Birmingham Hodge Hill and Solihull North – from the cost of living to children’s services, schools, transport and neighbourhood renewal. Here are the key wins for our area: Cost of living support £150 off energy bills next year, plus the expansion of the Warm Home Discount to help 6 million households. Freeze on NHS prescription charges and the first freeze in nationally-regulated rail...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: BIRMINGHAM PUB BOMBINGS: BYRNE URGES MINISTER TO RETHINK ICRIR REFERRAL
item.description: Liam Byrne, MP for Hodge Hill and Solihull North, has written to the Minister for Security to reject the proposal that the Birmingham Pub Bombings be referred to the Independent Commission for Reconciliation and Information Recovery (ICRIR) rather than dealt with through a statutory public inquiry. Mr Byrne has pressed the Minister to establish a judge-led statutory public inquiry into the Birmingham Pub Bombings under the 1995 Inquiries Act. Mr Byrne, said: “Having met with the Justice 4...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Byrne Presses HS2 to Deliver Business Park Timetable
item.description: Liam Byrne MP today met senior HS2 officials at the Washwood Heath Drew’s Lane site to insist on a firm timetable for handing back land to unlock the creation of thousands of local jobs. The Washwood Heath site (formerly the LDV site) has long been earmarked for redevelopment as a major business park, forming a key part of Liam Byrne’s election pledge to drive jobs and regeneration in East Birmingham. However, progress has been delayed due to unresolved issues with HS2. During today’s visit,...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: BIRMINGHAM PUB BOMBING STATEMENT
item.description: My statement following the decision to deny the families of the Birmingham Pub Bombing victims an independent, judge-led public inquiry.
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Tackling Speeding on Garretts Green Lane, Meadway, Lea Hall Road and Sheldon Heath Road
item.description: Following community consultations with residents over the past 12 months, speeding has been identified as the number one priority in the constituency. As such, I have organised a quarterly 'Speeding Summit', bringing together Birmingham City Council's Transport Planning team, West Midlands Police's Road Harm team, and the Road Safety Commissioner from the Mayor's Office. We have identified 20 priority roads, including Garretts Green Lane, Meadway, Lea Hall Road and Sheldon Heath Road in your...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Tackling Speeding on Eastfield, Little Bromwich and Corbyn Roads
item.description: Following community consultations with residents over the past 12 months, speeding has been identified as the number one priority in the constituency. As such, I have organised a quarterly 'Speeding Summit', bringing together Birmingham City Council's Transport Planning team, West Midlands Police's Road Harm team, and the Road Safety Commissioner from the Mayor's Office. We have identified 20 priority roads, including Eastfield, Little Bromwich and Corbyn Roads in your area - and I am working...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Tackling Speeding on Washwood Heath Road, Drews Lane, and around St Margaret's Church
item.description: Following community consultations with residents over the past 12 months, speeding has been identified as the number one priority in the constituency. As such, I have organised a quarterly 'Speeding Summit', bringing together Birmingham City Council's Transport Planning team, West Midlands Police's Road Harm team, and the Road Safety Commissioner from the Mayor's Office. Washwood Heath Road will be receiving Average Speed Cameras, which are set to be installed in December 2025 On top of this,...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Tackling Speeding on Kitts Green, Audley, Flaxley, Wyndhurst and Tile Cross Roads
item.description: Following community consultations with residents over the past 12 months, speeding has been identified as the number one priority in the constituency. As such, I have organised a quarterly 'Speeding Summit', bringing together Birmingham City Council's Transport Planning team, West Midlands Police's Road Harm team, and the Road Safety Commissioner from the Mayor's Office. We have identified 20 priority roads, including Kitts Green, Audley, Flaxley, Wyndhurst and Tile Cross roads in your area -...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: Sorting Out Parking Around Glebe Farm Shops
item.description: Following the announcement that we have won £20 million for Glebe Farm, I was out visiting the Mosque on Farmcote Road, along with Councillor Marje Bridle, to see how we can work together with the local community to improve the parking provision in the area.
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: £20 MILLION SECURED FOR GLEBE FARM INVESTMENT! HOW WOULD YOU USE THAT MONEY? TELL ME BELOW.
item.description: On 25th September, we received the fantastic news that £20 million has been secured for Glebe Farm as part of the Government's Plan for...
item.dc:creator: Liam Byrne MP

item.title: BYRNE SECURES £20MILLION GOVERNMENT INVESTMENT FOR GLEBE FARM REGENERATION
item.description: Liam Byrne MP has secured £20 million in new and extra funding for Glebe Farm, as part of the Government’s Plan for Neighbourhoods fund ....
item.dc:creator: oliviacoyle97
